expectations.text: Response contains required substrings (ClusterlessDecodingSelection, decoding_param_name, decoding_pipeline.md).
expectations.evidence: All required substrings present (decoding_pipeline.md mentioned in 'see the Spyglass decoding pipeline notebook / docs (decoding_pipeline.md in the Spyglass tutorials)').

expectations.text: Enumerates at least four distinct direct-FK divergence surfaces
expectations.evidence: All five direct-FK surfaces enumerated as items 1-5 (waveform features group, position group, decoding parameters, encoding interval, decoding interval) plus the estimate_decoding_params bool.

expectations.text: Recommends fetching and diffing ClusterlessDecodingSelection & key as the minimal isolating diagnostic
expectations.evidence: Section 'The minimal isolating diagnostic': `(ClusterlessDecodingSelection & key).fetch(as_dict=True)` field-by-field diff.

expectations.text: Does not commit to one cause before the diagnostic is run
expectations.evidence: 'Don't speculate which of the six surfaces above is responsible — just diff the selection row directly'.

expectations.text: Distinguishes direct-FK surfaces from upstream-chain differences
expectations.evidence: 'Second-order: upstream-chain differences' subsection explicitly: 'they are a second-order check: they matter only if the direct selection keys above already match'.

expectations.text: Does not misattribute divergence purely to environment / package drift
expectations.evidence: 'I would not jump to environment / package drift ... as the primary explanation. Spyglass's design intent is that the selection primary keys gate determinism'.
